varieties.name: Vanilla planifolia (Australian Accessions)
varieties.origin: Originally Mexico/Central America, cultivated in Australia since 1800s
varieties.traits: Climbing vine to 10-15m, fleshy green stems, oblong leaves 10-25cm, greenish-yellow flowers 5cm diameter
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to high with proper care, hand pollination required, flowering November-December
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery (Brisbane), All Rare Herbs (Australia-wide), local orchid societies

varieties.name: Queensland Commercial Strain
varieties.origin: Developed from Daintree, Bloomfield, and Sunshine Coast growers, 1990s-present
varieties.traits: Selected for vigorous growth in subtropical conditions, disease resistance
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity adapted to Queensland growing season
varieties.availability: Daintree Vanilla, Bloomfield Vanilla, Vanilla Vine Sunshine Coast, Broken Nose Vanilla (Cairns region)

varieties.name: Bourbon-type (Madagascar Lineage)
varieties.origin: Madagascar/Bourbon Islands, introduced 1822 from Mexican stock
varieties.traits: Long slender vines, dark green leaves, traditional planifolia characteristics
varieties.availability: Australian orchid nurseries, online specialty suppliers

varieties.name: Mansa Type
varieties.traits: Traditional Mexican variety, vigorous growth, broad leaves, thick stems

varieties.name: Variegated Vanilla planifolia
varieties.traits: Ornamental foliage with cream/white variegation on leaves, slower growing
varieties.yield_notes: Lower than standard varieties, grown primarily for aesthetics
varieties.availability: Orchid specialty nurseries, online orchid retailers

varieties.name: Vanilla tahitensis (Tahitian Vanilla)
varieties.origin: French Polynesia (Tahiti, Raiatea), hybrid of V. planifolia × V. odorata, 1800s
varieties.traits: Shorter, plumper pods, more robust plant, different flowering pattern
varieties.yield_notes: Lower than planifolia but indehiscent pods (don't split)
varieties.availability: Limited in Australia, specialty importers, Pacific Island connections

varieties.name: Vanilla pompona (West Indian Vanilla)
varieties.origin: West Indies, Central and South America, Mexico to Brazil
varieties.traits: Larger, rounder pods, broader oval leaves, more robust plant
varieties.yield_notes: Lower pod production but larger individual pods, more disease resistant
varieties.availability: Very limited in Australia, international specialty suppliers

varieties.name: Indian Vanilla planifolia (Kerala Accessions)
varieties.origin: Introduced to India 1835, adapted to Western Ghats conditions since mid-1900s
varieties.traits: Well-adapted to Kerala monsoon climate, vigorous growth in humid conditions
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity in Kerala conditions, 250-350 kg green pods per hectare
varieties.availability: Spices Board of India, Kerala Agricultural University, Goodness Vanilla (Western Ghats), ICAR-Indian Institute of Spices Research (Kozhikode), local vanilla growers associations

varieties.name: Wayanad Traditional Type
varieties.origin: Wayanad district, Kerala, cultivated since 1960s at Horticultural Research Station
varieties.traits: Selected for Wayanad's high elevation (700-2,100m) and cool, humid climate
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity in Kerala highland conditions
varieties.availability: Wayanad Horticultural Research Station, Kerala Agricultural University, local growers in Wayanad

varieties.name: Western Ghats Selection (Goodness Vanilla)
varieties.origin: Developed through Indian Vanilla Initiative across Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, and Kerala
varieties.traits: Selected for Western Ghats terroir, high quality production, organic cultivation practices
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable commercial production with organized cultivation network
varieties.availability: Indian Vanilla Initiative, Goodness Vanilla (Pollachi), Indian Vanilla Enterprise, Kerala contract farming networks

varieties.name: Karnataka-Kerala Border Type
varieties.traits: Adapted to transition zone between Karnataka and Kerala climates
varieties.availability: Karnataka and Kerala vanilla growers, regional agricultural departments

varieties.name: Nilgiri Type (Tamil Nadu-Kerala)
varieties.origin: Nilgiri region, research began 1945 at Kallar Fruit Research Station
varieties.availability: Tamil Nadu agricultural universities, accessible to Kerala growers

varieties.name: Bourbon-type (Indian Ocean Lineage)
varieties.origin: Madagascar/Réunion lineage, same V. planifolia grown in Bourbon Islands
varieties.traits: Traditional planifolia characteristics, proven tropical adaptation
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity in tropical monsoon conditions

varieties.name: Mexican Type (Available in India)
varieties.origin: Original Mexican planifolia, some imports and selections available

varieties.name: Vanilla tahitensis (Tahitian Type)
varieties.origin: French Polynesia, hybrid species, potentially available from Indonesia/Papua New Guinea
varieties.traits: Shorter, plumper pods, floral characteristics, different from planifolia
varieties.yield_notes: Lower than planifolia but indehiscent pods
varieties.availability: Very limited in India, research institutions, potential imports from Indonesia

varieties.name: Vanilla pompona (West Indian Type)
varieties.traits: More disease resistant, larger pods, broader leaves
varieties.availability: Extremely limited in India, requires specialized importation

varieties.name: Indonesian Planifolia Types
varieties.traits: Developed for Indonesian tropical conditions, certified varieties
